scenarios.description: Create a wave execution plan for a CLI refactor with 4 dependency levels. Tests DAG construction, parallel/sequential labelling, branch strategy, verification checklists, and correct output path.
instructions_covered: Wave 1 = tasks with no dependencies; Wave N topological sort
Build explicit dependency DAG before assigning waves
Every task appears in exactly one wave
Parallel vs sequential wave labelling
Write output to .context/plans/<slug>.md
Include dependency graph and branch strategy
Include verification checklist per wave

scenarios.description: Create a wave plan from a flat task list with explicit dependencies. Tests that the agent respects stated deps, does not invent extra constraints, and assigns parallel labels only where genuinely independent.
instructions_covered: Build explicit dependency DAG before assigning waves
Wave N topological sort from stated dependencies
No dependent tasks in the same wave
Every task appears in exactly one wave
NEVER invent dependencies — parallel is default when none stated
Parallel vs sequential labelling
Include verification checklist per wave
Branch strategy one branch per parallel workstream

scenarios.description: Mode B update: tick completed Wave 1 tasks and verifications, mark the wave done, update Wave 2 to in-progress, mark T2.1 in-progress, write the file back, and announce the next wave.
instructions_covered: Mode B: run verification commands and tick passing checks
Mode B: update Status column cells
Mode B: mark wave heading DONE when all verifications pass
Mode B: announce next wave and its execution mode
Mode B: write the updated document back
Wave document is the single source of truth — no status in task files

scenarios.description: Mode B gate enforcement: user asks to start Wave 2 while Wave 1 has an unconfirmed verification check. Agent must block advancement, identify the specific failing check, and explain the gate rule.
instructions_covered: NEVER advance to Wave N+1 before Wave N verification passes
Include Verification checklist per wave

scenarios.description: Mode A from open-ended prose: extract tasks from a natural-language description, build the DAG, assign waves via topological sort, and emit the wave document to the correct path.
instructions_covered: Wave 1 = tasks with no dependencies; Wave N topological sort
Build explicit dependency DAG before assigning waves
Every task appears in exactly one wave
Parallel vs sequential wave labelling
Write output to .context/plans/<slug>.md
Include dependency graph and branch strategy
Include verification checklist per wave
NEVER invent dependencies — parallel is default when none stated
Branch strategy one branch per parallel workstream

scenarios.description: Mode A edge case (stranded consumer pattern): a plan where a naive topological sort would separate infrastructure removal from service consumer code cleanup into different waves, but deploying the infrastructure wave alone would break the live service at runtime. Tests that the agent detects the hidden runtime dependency and groups all three phases into one must-land-together wave.
instructions_covered: NEVER create an unsafe intermediate deploy state at a wave boundary
Every wave boundary must be a safe deploy point
must-land-together wave label and atomic deploy note
Runtime dependency reasoning beyond the code-level DAG

uncovered_instructions: Premature consumer: resource creation must precede or land-together with consumer code
Breaking contract change: use expand-contract sequencing across waves
